sourceMovie.title: Audition
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Audition for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Takashi Miike keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Antichrist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Audition clicked for you, Antichrist (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Audition?
faq.answer: Try Antichrist, The Last House on the Left, The Devil's Rejects—each shares DNA with Audition while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Audition?
faq.answer: Audition (2000) is best described as Horror, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Audition worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
